vitals.date_specific: 2026: June 21 (6th edition, held; 2026 long course was replaced on the day by the medium course due to an Orange Heat Alert — see eligibility notes)
route_options: Long course (competitive, timed with age-group classification): 151 km / 4,172 m — Col de la Morte, Col d'Ornon, Alpe d'Huez, finish climb to Vaujany
Medium course (normally timed but non-competitive, alphabetical results): 60.8 km

climate.description: Typical mid-June Alps: mild days, cool summits; prepare for rain and wind.

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 74. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 70.

course_description.character: GFNY's Alpine chapter in the Oisans massif, built around one of cycling's most famous climbs. The long course opens with roughly 30 km of descending from Vaujany toward Séchilienne before Col de la Morte (15.1 km at 6.7% average, 10% max) starts the climbing; Col d'Ornon follows, then the course tackles L'Alpe d'Huez itself — the 21 hairpins straight from the Tour de France — before descending through Villard-Reculas and Pas de la Confession to a final, deceptive ~5 km kicker into Vaujany with pitches up to 14%. The planned long-course figures are 151 km / 4,172 m (raced in 2025, planned for 2026); a stale 170.6 km / 3,962 m figure previously on file was not supported by the GFNY sources reviewed and has been corrected. Know before you register: GFNY's medium course (60.8 km) normally gets a finish time but non-competitive, alphabetical-only results — standard GFNY two-tier format. 2026 was an exception worth flagging: an Orange Heat Alert forced organizers to put every rider, long-course and medium-course alike, onto the ~60 km medium route (roughly 2,000 m of climbing) — and for that one edition it was run fully timed with overall classification and podiums, not the usual alphabetical listing. Treat published course figures as edition-specific until you've checked the current year's actual route.
course_description.signature_challenge: L'Alpe d'Huez's 21 switchbacks arriving after Col de la Morte and Col d'Ornon have already taken a bite out of your legs — then a final, unglamorous ~5 km climb into Vaujany with 14% pitches that punishes anyone who celebrated too early on the Alpe.
suffering_zones: Col de la Morte: 15.1 km at 6.7% average, 10% max — a long grinding first climb after the opening descent
Col d'Ornon: shorter, ramps to 7% — positioned as a legs-testing bridge before Alpe d'Huez
L'Alpe d'Huez: the 21 hairpins, roughly 12 km at over 8% — the course's centerpiece and headline climb
Vaujany final climb: a deceptive ~5 km kicker with pitches up to 14% arriving after the Alpe d'Huez descent — punishes riders who spent everything on the famous climb

logistics.lodging_strategy: Abundant chalets and hotels in Vaujany and nearby Alpe d'Huez; GFNY offers camping and VIP options.
logistics.transport: GFNY-organized event with long competitive course (151km/4172m) and medium non-competitive (60km); starts/finishes in Vaujany with aid stations and perks.

history.origin_story: GFNY chapter launched in Vaujany in 2021 (confirmed 1st edition via GFNY's own 2021 race guide) built around L'Alpe d'Huez; ran its 6th edition on 21 June 2026. A separate, differently-routed GFNY Vaujany-area event (GFNY Alpes Vaujany – Croix de Fer, dating to 2020) is a distinct race with its own history — GFNY currently lists it without a 2026 date, a status unrelated to this event's.
notable_moments: 2026 (6th edition): an Orange Heat Alert forced organizers to put the entire field onto the medium (~60 km) course instead of the planned 151 km long course — for that one edition only, the substitute course was run fully timed with overall classification, not GFNY's usual alphabetical medium-course format

biased_opinion.summary: GFNY La Vaujany – L'Alpe d'Huez is a six-edition entry in the global GFNY series (founded 2021), and its long course delivers exactly what the name promises: Col de la Morte, Col d'Ornon, and a full ascent of L'Alpe d'Huez's 21 hairpins, capped by a nasty, unglamorous kicker into Vaujany. The long course carries GFNY's standard age-group classification; the medium course (60.8 km) gets a finish time but non-ranked, alphabetical results — a clearly disclosed two-tier format. What you need to know before registering: this is a weather-exposed Alpine event, and the 2026 edition (21 June) proved it — an Orange Heat Alert led organizers to scrap the long course entirely and put every rider on the ~60 km medium route, run fully timed with overall classification for that one year, but not the Alpe d'Huez long-course day riders signed up for. It's also easy to confuse with a second, separately-listed GFNY event sharing the same town — GFNY Alpes Vaujany – Croix de Fer, which currently has no 2026 date on GFNY's calendar; prior conflicting audits in our own research trail turned out to be tracking these two different events.
strengths: Well-documented long-course climbing: Col de la Morte, Col d'Ornon, and a full L'Alpe d'Huez ascent (21 switchbacks) on Tour de France-proven roads, confirmed against GFNY's own course page and multiple dated race reports
Clearly disclosed two-tier format — long course competitive with age-group classification, medium course explicitly non-ranked despite receiving a finish time
Six editions deep as of 2026 (founded 2021) with a checkable results archive rather than a one-off or speculative listing
Small-town Alpine base in Vaujany with lodging at multiple budget levels, close to Alpe d'Huez itself
weaknesses: Weather-exposed: the 2026 edition lost its entire long course to an Orange Heat Alert, meaning riders who signed up for the Alpe d'Huez long-course experience got the ~60 km medium route instead — a documented risk of this late-June Alpine date

Easy to confuse with a second, differently-routed GFNY event in the same town (GFNY Alpes Vaujany – Croix de Fer), which GFNY currently lists without a 2026 date and third-party calendars disagree about — check which race you're actually registering for
A regional Alpine chapter, not one of GFNY's marquee stops — our prestige rating for it is 2/5

Published course figures have varied by source and edition (151 km/4,172 m is the best-supported long-course figure in GFNY's own releases; older listings cited different, inconsistent numbers) — confirm the current year's actual route before banking on a specific distance
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A GFNY chapter with a full L'Alpe d'Huez ascent at its core — worth doing for the climb alone, but go in knowing Alpine heat scrapped the long course once already, and double-check you're registering for THIS event, not the separate Alpes Vaujany–Croix de Fer race sharing the same town.

final_verdict.one_liner: A GFNY chapter built on a full L'Alpe d'Huez ascent — clearly disclosed two-tier format, documented Alpine heat risk.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you want a competitively-classified ride up L'Alpe d'Huez's 21 switchbacks in a clearly disclosed GFNY two-tier event. Go in aware that heat forced the 2026 edition onto the shorter medium course — pack for variability, and confirm you're registering for this event and not the separate, differently-routed Alpes Vaujany–Croix de Fer race in the same town.
final_verdict.alternatives: For the same Alpe d'Huez climb without heat-alert risk of a shortened course: ride the climb self-supported, any time. For a bigger GFNY stop: GFNY's flagship World Championship events. For a nearby, differently-branded Alps cyclosportive: check the current status of GFNY Alpes Vaujany – Croix de Fer directly with GFNY before assuming it's running.

eligibility.notes: CONTRADICTION RESOLVED (2026-07-24): the June 21 GFNY La Vaujany – L'Alpe d'Huez edition definitely ran — its 6th edition, confirmed by GFNY's official June 24, 2026 post-race report (an Orange Heat Alert forced the long course to be replaced with a fully-timed medium course, but the event held and is active, not cancelled). The conflicting 2026-07-23 'cancelled' audit on the duplicate profile (gfny-la-vaujany-alpe-dhuez.json) was incorrect for the event it was attached to: its cited source (CycloWorld ID 40542, 'gfny-alpes-vaujany') tracks a separate, differently-routed race, GFNY Alpes Vaujany – Croix de Fer. That second event's 2026 status is unresolved-leaning-cancelled: GFNY's Race Finder lists it without a date, Vaujany's official summer agenda omits it, and third-party calendars conflict (one flags its provisional Aug 23 edition cancelled, another still shows it confirmed); no official GFNY cancellation announcement was found. Either way it does not affect this profile. Full research trail in research-dumps/la-vaujany.md.

catalog_flags.status_note: DEDUPE + CONTRADICTION RESOLUTION (2026-07-24): confirmed canonical over gfny-la-vaujany-alpe-dhuez.json, which is the same event (GFNY La Vaujany – L'Alpe d'Huez) and has been marked duplicate_of this slug. The two profiles' prior eligibility blocks (both dated 2026-07-23, one 'active' one 'cancelled') were reconciled this wave: the 'cancelled' audit was wrong for the profile it was attached to — its source (CycloWorld ID 40542) describes a separate GFNY event, GFNY Alpes Vaujany – Croix de Fer, whose own 2026 status remains unresolved (no date on GFNY's calendar; third-party listings conflict). This profile's 2026 edition (21 June, 6th edition) held, with a heat-alert course substitution. Course vitals also corrected this wave: long-course distance/elevation updated from a stale 170.6km/3,962m (unsupported in the GFNY sources reviewed) to 151km/4,172m per GFNY's dated 2025/2026 releases — the correct figure was already present, uncorrected, in this file's own logistics.transport field before this edit. Sol review note: a live Vaujany tourism page (vaujany.com/en/news/gfny-la-vaujany/) cross-contaminates the two events' copy (attaches the June 21 date to a Croix de Fer course description) — do not source from it.
